concerns.text: The plan assumes the existence of a pre-trained 'SiT-XL/2 with DAR' model on HuggingFace (e.g., 'llmXive/sit-xl-2-dar'). However, the 'Rethinking Cross-Layer Information Routing' paper describes a novel architecture modification (DAR) that is not part of the standard 'google/sit-xl-2' release. No public, pre-trained checkpoint with DAR enabled is currently known to exist on HuggingFace. The plan treats this as a 'verified' resource, but if the model weights do not exist, the entire 'Trace' phase (FR-001) is impossible to execute as described. The plan must either cite a specific, verified repository containing these weights or acknowledge that the 'Trace' phase requires training a DAR model from scratch (which violates the CPU/6-hour constraints).

The plan cites 'imagenet-1k' via HuggingFace streaming as the primary dataset. While the dataset exists, the plan assumes a 100-image subset is sufficient for FID calculation. FID (Fréchet Inception Distance) is a statistical metric that requires a large sample size (typically thousands of images) to converge and be meaningful. Calculating FID on only 100 images will result in high variance and statistically invalid results, rendering the 'Benchmark' phase (FR-003) unable to answer the research question regarding quality degradation. The plan must either increase the sample size (which may violate disk/CPU constraints) or switch to a metric valid for small samples (e.g., Precision/Recall with bootstrapping, or a different evaluation protocol).
